title: `podman commit` fails when --userns, --uidmap, or --gidmap specified
body:
**Kind**
bug

**Description**

When committing changes to a running container started with a a specified namespace using --uidmap, --gidmap, or --userns the command fails with the error:
```
Error: error copying layers and metadata for container <CONTAINER_ID>":
Error initializing source containers-storage:<CONTAINER-NAME>: 
unable to look up size of layer "<STORAGE_ID>"
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run a container specifying a namespace using --uidmap, --gidmap, or --userns
2. Attempt to commit said image


**Rootless:**
```
podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id --name test alpine
```

```
podman commit test test

Error: error copying layers and metadata for container
"46ec4dd4a4c0213ff8baf2e589199ddc2263a1efdd71ec85f2102f8fbe5eee9b": 
Error initializing source containers-storage:test: 
unable to look up size of layer
"1c1809d86870c4b3c20557351fe3fb43d6fd803b7d643066ebf62adb0c8f0290"
```
**Describe the results you received:**

**Rootfull:**
```
sudo podman run -it --rm --uidmap 0:0:999999 --name test alpine
```

```
sudo podman commit test test

Error: error copying layers and metadata for container
"3133e52bdc717a2327e8c921686174fc4997006e86fc9652bb83ae3e44e8b068": 
Error initializing source containers-storage:test: unable to look up size of layer
"f45690e905efebc8d849b3f844e412941870be561101a0b3f1d9cfb7a075bb04"
```

**Without specifying a namespace works fine:**
```
podman run -it --rm --name test alpine
```

```
podman commit test test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ace0eda3e3be skipped: already exists
Copying blob a7686bbd68ad done
Copying config e8c4f168b5 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
e8c4f168b5562ee2a7418251dee06df3028b74d7c03fe4ab5d95c6fb31852303
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container to successfullly commit when running in a namespace

**Additional information you deem important**

I need to specify a namespace to be able to write to mounted volumes, and sometimes it's necessary to commit changes made to running containers without rebuilding from scratch.

I searched the issue history and documentation but did not turn up anything.  If this is expected behavior it would be great to know the underlying reason and posible workaround. 

The storage driver is **btrfs**
I've tried turning off the index for the overlayfs driver 
```
[storage.options.overlay] 
index=off
```
 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Git Commit:   db1d2ff111ee9b012779ff3a5279a982520ccda4
Built:        Tue Dec  1 16:59:35 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Package info**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical

**Thank you!**

comments.body:
could you confirm it works with overlay?

The btrfs storage driver is not as stable and widely used as overlay.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no response, I am going to close this issue.  Reopen if more information is to be provided.

I can confirm this does not work with overlay, and may be the same as this:?

https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2800

Error: error copying layers and metadata for container "047c2f8b4702bc5bf5dee497db0172131ff6a1a8bf3ce70309e3e24d89e07747": Error initializing source containers-storage:company-wordpress: unable to look up size of layer "d35e2fdeb713e8779a404b23600553dc68553657d292ed0597991be59face74e"
